2604.14991.title: Predicting Power-System Dynamic Trajectories with Foundation Models
2604.14991.paper_summary: As power systems transition toward renewable-rich and inverter-dominated operations, accurate time-domain dynamic analysis becomes increasingly critical. Such analysis supports key operational tasks, including transient stability assessment, dynamic security analysis, contingency screening, and post-fault trajectory evaluation. In practice, these tasks may operate under several challenges, including unknown and time-varying system parameters, privacy constraints on data sharing, and the need for fast online inference. Existing learning-based approaches are typically trained for individual systems and therefore lack generalization across operating conditions and physical parameters. Hence, this paper proposes LArge Scale Small ODE (LASS)-ODE-Power, a learning framework for general-purpose time-domain prediction. The proposed approach leverages large-scale pretraining on more than 40 GB of DAE or ordinary differential-equation (ODE) trajectories to learn transferable representations. The resulting model supports trajectory prediction from short measurement prefixes across diverse dynamic regimes, including electromechanical and inverter-driven systems. Hence, the model can be directly used without data sharing in a zero-shot setting. In addition, the proposed architecture incorporates parallel and linearized computation to achieve fast inference. Moreover, to enhance task-specific performance in power systems, a specialized fine-tuning strategy is developed based on approximately 1 GB of heterogeneous power-system dynamic data. Extensive experiments over diverse power-system simulation scenarios demonstrate that LASS-ODE-Power consistently outperforms existing learning-based models in trajectory prediction accuracy with efficient inference.
2604.14991.paper_summary_zh: 隨著電力系統向豐富可再生能源和以逆變器為主的運行過渡，準確的時域動態分析變得越來越重要。這種分析支持關鍵的操作任務，包括暫態穩定性評估、動態安全分析、應急篩選和故障後軌跡評估。實際上，這些任務可能面臨多種挑戰，包括未知和隨時間變化的系統參數、數據共享的隱私限制，以及快速在線推斷的需求。現有的基於學習的方法通常是針對單個系統進行訓練，因此在操作條件和物理參數之間缺乏泛化能力。因此，本文提出了LArge Scale Small ODE (LASS)-ODE-Power，這是一個通用時域預測的學習框架。所提出的方法利用超過40 GB的DAE或常微分方程（ODE）軌跡進行大規模預訓練，以學習可轉移的表示。所得模型支持從短測量前綴進行軌跡預測，涵蓋多種動態範疇，包括電機和逆變器驅動系統。因此，該模型可以在零樣本設置中直接使用，而無需數據共享。此外，所提出的架構結合了並行和線性化計算，以實現快速推斷。此外，為了增強電力系統中特定任務的性能，基於約1 GB的異構電力系統動態數據開發了一種專門的微調策略。對各種電力系統模擬場景進行的廣泛實驗表明，LASS-ODE-Power在軌跡預測準確性和高效推斷方面始終優於現有的基於學習的模型。
2604.14991.author: Haoran Li et.al.
2604.14991.authors: Haoran Li, Lihao Mai, Chenhan Xiao, Erik Blasch, Yang Weng
